result:
Perfect! All tests pass. Here's a summary of the bugs I fixed:

`‚òÖ Insight ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ`
The bugs fell into three categories: **logic inversions** (comparing wrong direction), **operator mistakes** (wrong arithmetic operator), and **data mutations** (when to delete vs. keep). In inventory systems, these types of errors are especially dangerous because they silently corrupt data‚Äîa discount that multiplies instead of reducing, or a low-stock check inverted so it alerts on well-stocked items. The test suite caught all of them by verifying expected values precisely.
`‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ`

**Fixed bugs:**

1. **`inventory.py:27`** ‚Äì `remove_item`: Changed line 29 from `raise InsufficientStockError(name)` to `del self.items[name]` so items are fully removed when quantity reaches zero.
2. **`inventory.py:38`** ‚Äì `total_value`: Changed `+` to `*` so it multiplies qty and price instead of adding them.
3. **`inventory.py:45`** ‚Äì `low_stock`: Changed `>` to `<=` so it correctly returns items at or below the threshold.
4. **`inventory.py:52`** ‚Äì `apply_discount`: Changed `item["price"] * (pct / 100)` to `item["price"] * (1 - pct / 100)` so discounts reduce price, not erase it.
5. **`report.py:12`** ‚Äì `format_report`: Removed `reverse=True` from sort so items appear in ascending alphabetical order.
6. **`report.py:26`** ‚Äì `restock_list`: Changed `",".join(low)` to `", ".join(low)` to add space after commas.
7. **`report.py:35`** ‚Äì `summary`: Removed the `- 1` from item count calculation.
